old_claim.note: Theater Ticketing Systems is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Theater supplies the dramatic-performance and play-text tradition that printed scripts publish; paid ticketing systems are not required by the publication scope.

source_shape.source_locator: Overview and title-page entries: lists Shakespeare plays printed in quarto before the First Folio, with title pages describing staged performances by theater companies and printed or sold editions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early printed play quartos are explicitly theatrical performance texts and commercial printed editions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source ties printed scripts to theater-company performance and print sale, while not requiring ticketing systems as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Printed Theater Scripts directly depended on Theater Ticketing Systems, which blurred publication of play texts with paid-entry theater commerce.
ontology_after: Printed Theater Scripts now directly depends on Theater as the performance and dramatic-text lineage, while keeping printing press and printing-rights context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_theater_scripts no longer directly depends on theater_ticketing_systems and now directly depends on theater.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to commercial theater admission or box-office operations rather than printed scripts.
A source shows paid ticketing systems were necessary for the early publication and circulation of play texts.
The printing_press or early_copyright_printing_privileges contexts are removed without a narrower replacement.
short_reason: Printed scripts require theater and print publication, not ticketing infrastructure.
